children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “Connecting Oceania’s Nations with Vanguard Exercises and National Empowerment Act of 2023” or the “CONVENE Act of 2023”.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Relations and the Select Committee on Intelligence of the Senate;

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Affairs and the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: C
children.text: the congressional defense committees.

children.heading: .—National security council
children.label: 2 .—National security council
children.text: The term “national security council” means, with respect to a specified country, an intergovernmental body under the jurisdiction of the freely elected government of the specified country that acts as the primary coordinating entity for security cooperation, disaster response, and the activities described in subsection (c)(5).

children.label: A
children.text: the Federated States of Micronesia;

children.label: B
children.text: the Republic of the Marshall Islands; and

children.label: C
children.text: the Republic of Palau.

children.heading: National security councils of specified countries
children.label: c National security councils of specified countries
i
children.text: to better coordinate with the United States Government, including the Armed Forces, as appropriate;

children.label: ii
children.text: to increase cohesion on activities, including emergency humanitarian response, law enforcement, and maritime security activities; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to provide trained professionals to serve as members of the committees of the specified country established under the applicable Compact of Free Association; and

children.label: B
children.text: for the purpose of enhancing resilience capabilities and protecting the people, infrastructure, and territory of the specified country from malign actions.

children.heading: Composition
children.label: 2 Composition
children.text: The Secretary of State, respecting the unique needs of each specified country, may seek to ensure that the national security council, or other identified coordinating body, of the specified country is composed of sufficient staff and members to enable the activities described in paragraph (5).

children.label: A
children.text: the equipment and services used by the national security council or other identified coordinating body are compliant with security standards so as to minimize the risk of cyberattacks or espionage;

children.label: B
children.text: the national security council or other identified coordinating body takes all reasonable efforts not to procure or use systems, equipment, or software that originates from any entity identified under section 1260H of the William M. (Mac) Thornberry National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year ( note); and110 U.S.C. 113So in original. Probably should be followed by “2021”.1

children.label: C
children.text: to the extent practicable, the equipment and services used by the national security council or other identified coordinating body are interoperable with the equipment and services used by the national security councils, or other identified coordinating bodies, of the other specified countries.

children.label: i
children.text: an assessment as to whether a national security council or a similar formal coordinating body is helping or would help achieve the objectives described in paragraph (1) at acceptable financial and opportunity cost;

children.label: ii
children.text: a description of all actions taken by the United States Government to assist in the identification or maintenance of a national security council, or other identified coordinating body, in each specified country;

children.label: I
children.text: the specified country has appropriately staffed its national security council or other identified coordinating body; and

children.label: II
children.text: the extent to which the national security council, or other identified coordinating body, of the specified country is capable of carrying out the activities described in paragraph (5);

children.label: I
children.text: any challenge to cooperation and coordination with the national security council, or other identified coordinating body, of any specified country;

children.label: II
children.text: current efforts by the Secretary of State to coordinate with the specified countries on the activities described in paragraph (5); and

children.label: III
children.text: existing governmental entities within each specified country that are capable of supporting such activities;

children.label: I
children.text: the implementation of the national security council, or other identified coordinating body, of any specified country; and

children.label: II
children.text: the implementation of paragraphs (1) through (3);

children.label: vi
children.text: an assessment of any attempt or campaign by a malign actor to influence the political, security, or economic policy of a specified country, a member of a national security council or other identified coordinating body, or an immediate family member of such a member; and

children.label: vii
children.text: any other matter the Secretary of State considers relevant.

children.heading: Form
children.label: B Form
children.text: Each report required by subparagraph (A) may be submitted in unclassified form and may include a classified annex.

children.label: i
children.text: the prosecution and investigation of transnational criminal enterprises;

children.label: ii
children.text: responses to national emergencies, such as natural disasters;

children.label: iii
children.text: counterintelligence and counter- coercion responses to foreign threats; and

children.label: iv
children.text: efforts to combat illegal, unreported, or unregulated fishing.

children.label: B
children.text: Coordination with United States Government officials on humanitarian response, military exercises, law enforcement, and other issues of security concern.

children.label: C
children.text: Identification and development of an existing governmental entity to support homeland defense and civil support activities.
